steps.title: Expand the equation
steps.content:
First put the equation in standard quadratic form.
$$x(x+8)-13=0$$
$$x^2+8x-13=0$$
So $a=1$, $b=8$, and $c=-13$.

steps.title: Use the quadratic formula
steps.content: The discriminant is $$b^2-4ac=8^2-4(1)(-13)=64+52=116$$ Then $$x=\frac{-8\pm\sqrt{116}}{2}$$ The answer is D.
